publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap for Probabilistic graphical models concept; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Probabilistic graphical models concept: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? Every directed edge in a Bayesian network proves that changing the parent will cause the child to change.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Read the graph as a probabilistic factorization and conditional-independence claim; causal interpretation requires additional assumptions and evidence.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Read the graph as a probabilistic factorization and conditional-independence claim; causal interpretation requires additional assumptions and evidence.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.
